description: AHVI full outfit builder logic. Combines top, bottom, one-piece, layers, shoes, accessories, color harmony, occasion, season, body-balance, and styling mood into complete outfit outputs for women and men.
runtime_order: select outfit mode (top_bottom, one_piece, layered, indian, fusion)
apply body balance refinement if allowed

global_builder_rules.safe_option_definition: Most practical and occasion-safe formula with stable color harmony and easier shoe choice.
global_builder_rules.bold_option_definition: One stronger move only: color, silhouette, or accessory emphasis, while still respecting context.
global_builder_rules.one_hero_rule: Each outfit should usually have one hero decision only: statement color, statement layer, statement accessory, or statement shoe.
global_builder_rules.layer_rule: If the outfit already has texture, print, or embellishment, use cleaner layers.
global_builder_rules.shoe_rule: Shoes must match both formality and movement needs.
global_builder_rules.accessory_rule: Accessories should finish the outfit, not overcrowd it.
global_builder_rules.color_rule: Base formula picks the silhouette. Color logic upgrades the emotional feel.
global_builder_rules.bag_rule: Bag should match the polish level of shoes and outfit, not necessarily the exact color.
global_builder_rules.comfort_first_rule: If user signals comfort need, travel, mobility, or vulnerable mood, comfort outranks drama.

layer_logic: use light blazer if place is city or meeting-led
use cardigan if user tone or styling mood is softer
skip layer if weather is hot and outfit is already complete
body_balance_rules: use tuck or half_tuck if cleaner waist definition helps
prefer straighter bottoms if top has volume
prefer cleaner tops if bottom is wider or more detailed
comfort_rules: prefer sneakers or loafers for longer days
avoid fragile fabrics for travel-heavy contexts
builder_modes.output_shape: top + bottom + optional layer + shoe + bag + small accessory finisher
builder_modes.notes: Core builder for women’s daywear and casual polish.

layer_logic: prefer blazer when authority or structure is needed
prefer softer longline layer when user wants a gentler work look
body_balance_rules: use clean verticals for stronger work polish
keep one structured element near shoulders or waist for balance
comfort_rules: prefer loafers or block heels for long office days
avoid over-accessorising in formal work contexts
builder_modes.output_shape: work top + tailored bottom + blazer_optional + work shoe + structured bag + minimal jewellery
builder_modes.notes: Core office and professional builder for women.

layer_logic: use blazer if place is city-smart or meeting-adjacent
use cardigan if the look should stay softer
use no layer if dress is already the hero
body_balance_rules: use belt or waist emphasis only if the silhouette needs definition
keep accessories lighter if the dress print is active
comfort_rules: prefer flats or sneakers for longer daywear
avoid delicate heels for resort or walking-heavy days
builder_modes.output_shape: one_piece + optional layer + shoe + bag + light jewellery

layer_logic: use sharp blazer or shawl only if weather or venue requires it
avoid layers that reduce the clean evening line unless necessary
body_balance_rules: if earrings are statement, reduce necklace
if outfit is already high-shine, simplify accessories and shoes
comfort_rules: if user says no heels, route to pointed flats or dressy sandals
if event includes long standing, prefer stable heel shapes
builder_modes.output_shape: sleek base + dressy shoe + evening bag + controlled statement accessory
builder_modes.notes: Women’s evening and event outfit builder.

layer_logic: use dupatta as optional polish layer rather than mandatory weight
if kurta set is already coordinated, keep support styling lighter
body_balance_rules: keep bottoms cleaner if top has print or embroidery
use vertical dupatta drape for a cleaner longer line
comfort_rules: prefer juttis or sandals for long family events
avoid very heavy jewellery for daytime Indianwear
builder_modes.notes: Core Indian daywear builder for women.

layer_logic: use dupatta, festive jacket, or blouse detailing as the main celebration element, not all three at once
if outfit is heavily embellished, simplify shoes and jewellery weight
body_balance_rules: keep one focal area only: earrings, neckline, or dupatta
if skirt or lehenga has volume, keep upper accessories cleaner
comfort_rules: prefer block heels or juttis for long wedding events
avoid fragile shoes on uneven venues
builder_modes.output_shape: festive base + traditional shoe + festive bag + hero accessory + support accessory
builder_modes.notes: Women’s festive and wedding guest builder.

layer_logic: use overshirt if outfit needs shape but not formality
skip layer if climate is hot and base already feels complete
body_balance_rules: keep fit clean before adding accessory complexity
if top has texture, keep bottom cleaner
comfort_rules: prefer clean sneakers for long walking days
prefer loafers for smart casual polish without stiffness
builder_modes.output_shape: top + bottom + optional layer + shoe + watch_or_small_accessory

layer_logic: use blazer when sharper authority is needed
prefer shirt-trouser clarity before adding extra detail
body_balance_rules: clean fit is more important than accessory complexity
match belt with shoe when formality rises
comfort_rules: use loafers for softer workwear, dress shoes for sharper workwear
avoid casual sneakers in presentation-led contexts
builder_modes.output_shape: shirt + tailored bottom + blazer_optional + formal_shoe + belt + watch + work_bag
builder_modes.notes: Core office and business builder for men.

layer_logic: use unstructured jacket or overshirt if the look needs more polish
keep layering light in warm weather
body_balance_rules: use cleaner bottoms if top texture is prominent
avoid too many competing menswear details at once
comfort_rules: loafers increase polish, sneakers keep youthfulness and ease
builder_modes.output_shape: smart top + chino_or_clean_bottom + light_layer_optional + shoe + watch + bag_optional

layer_logic: use dark blazer only if it sharpens rather than overcomplicates
keep evening base sleek and low-noise
body_balance_rules: dark bottom anchors evening menswear most reliably
avoid overly casual shoes when the shirt or knit is already dressy
comfort_rules: loafers work when the mood is softer, formal shoes when the event is stricter
builder_modes.output_shape: sharp top + dark bottom + blazer_optional + evening_shoe + watch

layer_logic: coat or heavier outer layer becomes a primary visual anchor
winter outfits can take deeper color combinations and more texture
body_balance_rules: boots or stronger footwear help anchor heavier top layers
keep accessories tighter when outerwear already adds presence
comfort_rules: prioritise warmth, traction, and shoe durability in winter contexts

layer_logic: use either jacket emphasis or stole emphasis, not both as equal heroes
keep the festive structure clean
body_balance_rules: traditionalwear reads best when silhouette and accessories stay controlled
if kurta set is already rich in color, keep shoes and add-ons simpler
comfort_rules: prefer mojris or loafers that can last through long family functions
builder_modes.notes: Core Indian festive builder for men.

safe_generation_checks: Do not combine more than one bold decision unless explicitly requested.
Do not output impractical shoes for travel-heavy or walking-heavy events.
Do not force body-balance language if user has not asked for it or is emotionally vulnerable.
Do not repeat the same base formula across all three options.
quality_checks: Each outfit must feel complete, not just descriptive.
Each outfit must include a finishing logic: shoe, accessory, or layer.
Color should explain mood, not only matching.
If a layer is present, it must improve context, weather fit, or polish.

dev_notes: This file is the orchestration layer for the styling brain.
It should not replace formula banks; it should assemble them.
Base formula decides outfit skeleton. Color decides emotional effect. Shoes decide practicality and polish. Accessories decide finish.
Use 2 safe + 1 bold by default unless user asks for a different format.
Future versions can add explicit weather, age, body-type, and wardrobe-inventory routing.
